title: Spacers: Space Adventure Board Game (Xia: Faster. Smaller. Better?)
body:
In this video, we take an in-depth look at “Spacers,” a space adventure board game that offers a streamlined, faster-paced experience compared to the popular game, Zia. You play as a “spacer,” a freelance space traveler in a new solar system, trying to gain fame through various activities like trading, combat, and exploration.

Highlights of the Game:

  • Random Encounters: The game features random encounter cards that provide narrative-driven choices, adding depth and replayability.
  • Missions: Players can undertake missions that require them to transport goods, engage in combat, or explore the galaxy, each with its own unique challenges and rewards.
  • Components: The game includes a modular star map, various ships, a status board, and planet features that change each game, ensuring no two games are alike.
  • Solo Mode: The solo mode is robust, with specific rules and objectives, providing a satisfying challenge for solo players.

The game was initially crowdfunded but didn’t succeed. However, it’s now available on Gamecrafter, and the designer is open to expanding the game with suggestions from the community.

Support the game! Spacers deserves a wider audience.
